proposal.signal_logic: Regime-aware momentum applied across multiple sleeves of 6 stocks each. Each sleeve is selected to share internal coherence characteristics (sector homogeneity, liquidity tier, beta profile, or thematic linkage) similar to the original AVGO/NVDA/AAPL/LLY/META/COST group. Within each sleeve, the same momentum signal logic from the Regime-Aware Momentum Frozen Reference Paper v1 is applied: measure trailing return over a lookback window, filter by a volatility cap, and gate all entries on a benchmark trend filter (SPY above its N-day moving average). Regime detection uses SPY trend as the macro gate. Each sleeve operates as an independent sub-portfolio with its own position sizing, so regime-appropriate sleeves are active while others are suppressed.

universe.description: Six initial sleeves of 6 stocks each, totaling up to 36 symbols. All symbols must be US-listed equities available on Alpaca with OHLCV history from at least 2020-01-01.

proposal.entry_rules: Identical to the Regime-Aware Momentum Frozen Reference Paper v1 entry logic, applied per sleeve independently.
proposal.exit_rules: Identical to the Regime-Aware Momentum Frozen Reference Paper v1 exit logic.

risk_model.description: Per-sleeve risk isolation with portfolio-level concentration guard.

acceptance_criteria.other: At least 4 of 6 sleeves must individually pass evidence thresholds. At least 2 non-original sleeves must show positive expectancy in at least one non-COVID-recovery era. No single sleeve may contribute more than 40% of total portfolio drawdown.

known_limitations: Sleeve compositions (B through F) are Talon initial proposals and have not been operator-confirmed. Operator must review and approve symbol lists before promotion.
Sleeve selection methodology is qualitative in v1 (sector/theme coherence). A quantitative screening process (e.g., minimum ADV, correlation filter, momentum factor loading) is deferred to v2.
Capital allocation across sleeves is equal-weight in v1. Dynamic regime-based sleeve weighting is a v2 enhancement.

COIN and AFRM in sleeve_C (Financials) have shorter listing histories and higher volatility than the original sleeve members. They may produce fewer qualifying signals and should be monitored for volatility filter suppression.
SNOW and MDB in sleeve_B (Cloud SaaS) IPO'd in late 2020; pre-2021 history is thin. The era_covid_recovery era will have reduced data for these symbols.

Cross-sleeve concentration risk: in strong bull regimes, tech-adjacent sleeves (A, B, F) may all activate simultaneously, reducing diversification benefit. The concentration guard in the risk model partially mitigates this but does not eliminate it.
The phase1_short window (2026 tape) is a plumbing/signal-wiring check only. Expected trade count is low and no standalone edge conclusion should be drawn from it.
All data dependencies are OHLCV-only (alpaca_equity_ohlcv). No exotic data sources are required. This is a deliberate fidelity choice to maintain the proven signal logic of the parent strategy.
Future sleeve additions or substitutions should re-run the full experiment_plan before promotion.

fields.title: Test x10
fields.thesis: The thesis is to expand upon the currently deployed live autonomous strategy for stocks:  Regime-Aware Momentum Frozen Reference Paper v1. Talon needs to mimic that entire strategy, with flexibility in mind for continuous improvements, but with the addition of many different groups of 6 stocks. Current we only trade AVGO, NVDA, AAPL, LLY, META and COS, but we should seek different sleeves. We need to keep in mind what this original group has in common to maintain fidelity between the 6 chosen stocks but this can increase our era robustness as certain groups may perform better under different market regimes. We will need to expand further on how to select stocks for the new groups but I’m curious to see what talon can produce from the beginning.

provenance.prompt:
You are Talon's durable spec-drafting worker inside the Vires Research Lab.
Return only the structured object requested by the schema. Do not invent backtest results.
The structured object has two string fields: proposal_json and assessment_json.
Each field must be valid JSON text, not markdown, not fenced code, and not comments.
proposal_json contract:
- proposal_json is not the full persisted StrategySpecV1. It is the Talon proposal payload that the server wraps into StrategySpecV1.
- Include these top-level fields exactly: signal_logic, entry_rules, exit_rules, risk_model, universe, required_data, experiment_plan, benchmark, acceptance_criteria.
- benchmark must be a string symbol such as "SPY".
- acceptance_criteria must be an object with numeric min_sharpe, max_drawdown_pct, and min_hit_rate_pct; optional other may be a string or null.
- experiment_plan must include benchmark, windows, runnable_eras, eras, evidence_thresholds, decisive_verdict_rules, and known_limitations.
- experiment_plan.benchmark must be an object such as {"symbol":"SPY","comparison_mode":"both"}.
- Do not include wrapper fields that the server supplies, such as schema_version, spec_id, idea_id, created_at, state, authoring_mode, or registered_strategy_id.
assessment_json must JSON.stringify the complete data-readiness assessment.

Your task has two parts:
1. Draft a StrategySpecV1 starting point for the operator to review.
2. Draft its experiment_plan: how the strategy will be judged before implementation.
3. Assess whether every data dependency is actually available in the catalog.
4. If reference strategies are supplied, use them as parent/context for NEW code and honor the operator's delta notes. Do not simply route the idea to a parent strategy.

Experiment-plan rules:
- The plan is part of the spec. Do not omit it.
- Use ISO dates for windows. If the idea does not specify dates, choose a recent executable window and state limitations.
- evidence_thresholds must be numeric. minimum_trade_count defaults to at least 5 for exploratory stock ideas unless the thesis justifies a higher floor.
- data_requirements are resolved by the server from your assessment; in experiment_plan focus on benchmark, windows, eras, thresholds, verdict rules, and limitations.
- If a strategy depends on seeded/current-only data, put that in known_limitations and make the plan clear that the first run validates plumbing, not historical edge.

Durable Talon strategy lessons:
- Ape Wisdom seeded-attention is proof plumbing unless historical attention exists: The first Ape Wisdom run proved the idea-to-job loop, but it did not prove a historical edge. The strategy used a 2026-04-14 seeded attention artifact, so only post-seed tape was honestly tradeable and the run produced one ASML trade.
  - If an idea depends on seeded/current-only data, label the first run as a plumbing/proof run unless historical data exists.
  - Separate config window, fresh usable tape, tradeable window, benchmark window, evaluated trading days, and trade count in the experiment plan.
  - Do not imply a strategy is judgeable when expected trades are below the readiness floor.
  - Ask for a broader universe, longer executable window, or missing historical connector when the plan cannot meet minimum evidence thresholds.
  - Put seeded-data limitations in experiment_plan.known_limitations.

Known-good / known-bad exemplars:
- Known-good regime-aware momentum template: Jacob and Codex previously found a regime-aware momentum pattern that beat SPY by roughly ten percentage points. Treat it as the first known-good template: simple OHLCV-derived momentum, explicit SPY benchmark, bounded stop/target sweeps, enough trades to judge, and clear readiness gates.
  - Prefer simple, testable signal logic over clever data dependencies when the idea does not require exotic data.
  - Include benchmark, eras/windows, trade-count floor, drawdown cap, and decisive verdict rules with the strategy draft.
  - Use available OHLCV-derived features as the baseline template before reaching for unavailable sentiment, macro, options, or on-chain data.
  - If the operator asks for a novel data source, first check whether an available proxy can honestly test the thesis.

- Any field with field_meta.locked=true is an operator decision. Do not contradict it.
- Fill missing fields only. If a locked field makes the strategy impossible, surface that honestly in limitations or data readiness.
- If the mode is advanced, avoid interrupting with inline clarifications unless the draft cannot proceed.

Data readiness rules:
- PASS only when every core requirement maps to AVAILABLE.
- WARN when requirements map to PARTIAL, or when a non-core nice-to-have is missing.
- BLOCKED when any core signal, entry rule, or exit rule requires missing data.
- When unsure whether data is core, treat it as core.
- Thin theses are allowed. Unavailable data is not allowed.

- One capability per underlying feed. Idea sleeve: STOCKS
Idea title: Test x10
Idea thesis: The thesis is to expand upon the currently deployed live autonomous strategy for stocks:  Regime-Aware Momentum Frozen Reference Paper v1. Talon needs to mimic that entire strategy, with flexibility in mind for continuous improvements, but with the addition of many different groups of 6 stocks. Current we only trade AVGO, NVDA, AAPL, LLY, META and COS, but we should seek different sleeves. We need to keep in mind what this original group has in common to maintain fidelity between the 6 chosen stocks but this can increase our era robustness as certain groups may perform better under different market regimes. We will need to expand further on how to select stocks for the new groups but I’m curious to see what talon can produce from the beginning.

raw_proposal.signal_logic: Regime-aware momentum applied across multiple sleeves of 6 stocks each. Each sleeve is selected to share internal coherence characteristics (sector homogeneity, liquidity tier, beta profile, or thematic linkage) similar to the original AVGO/NVDA/AAPL/LLY/META/COST group. Within each sleeve, the same momentum signal logic from the Regime-Aware Momentum Frozen Reference Paper v1 is applied: measure trailing return over a lookback window, filter by a volatility cap, and gate all entries on a benchmark trend filter (SPY above its N-day moving average). Regime detection uses SPY trend as the macro gate. Each sleeve operates as an independent sub-portfolio with its own position sizing, so regime-appropriate sleeves are active while others are suppressed.
raw_proposal.entry_rules: Identical to the Regime-Aware Momentum Frozen Reference Paper v1 entry logic, applied per sleeve independently.
raw_proposal.exit_rules: Identical to the Regime-Aware Momentum Frozen Reference Paper v1 exit logic.
raw_proposal.risk_model: Per-sleeve risk isolation with portfolio-level concentration guard.
raw_proposal.universe: Six initial sleeves of 6 stocks each, totaling up to 36 symbols. All symbols must be US-listed equities available on Alpaca with OHLCV history from at least 2020-01-01.

decisive_verdict_rules.pass: Passes only if it beats the benchmark after meeting the minimum trade-count and evaluated-days thresholds.
decisive_verdict_rules.inconclusive: {"condition":"Any sleeve produces < 30 trades in the full window OR profitable fold pct is between 40-50% for >= 2 sleeves. Action: extend test window by inconclusive_follow_up_window_days or substitute underperforming sleeve symbols."}
decisive_verdict_rules.fail: Fails if it underperforms the benchmark or breaches risk limits after evidence thresholds are met.

known_limitations: Sleeve compositions (B through F) are Talon initial proposals and have not been operator-confirmed. Operator must review and approve symbol lists before promotion.
Sleeve selection methodology is qualitative in v1 (sector/theme coherence). A quantitative screening process (e.g., minimum ADV, correlation filter, momentum factor loading) is deferred to v2.
Capital allocation across sleeves is equal-weight in v1. Dynamic regime-based sleeve weighting is a v2 enhancement.

COIN and AFRM in sleeve_C (Financials) have shorter listing histories and higher volatility than the original sleeve members. They may produce fewer qualifying signals and should be monitored for volatility filter suppression.
SNOW and MDB in sleeve_B (Cloud SaaS) IPO'd in late 2020; pre-2021 history is thin. The era_covid_recovery era will have reduced data for these symbols.

Cross-sleeve concentration risk: in strong bull regimes, tech-adjacent sleeves (A, B, F) may all activate simultaneously, reducing diversification benefit. The concentration guard in the risk model partially mitigates this but does not eliminate it.
The phase1_short window (2026 tape) is a plumbing/signal-wiring check only. Expected trade count is low and no standalone edge conclusion should be drawn from it.
All data dependencies are OHLCV-only (alpaca_equity_ohlcv). No exotic data sources are required. This is a deliberate fidelity choice to maintain the proven signal logic of the parent strategy.
Future sleeve additions or substitutions should re-run the full experiment_plan before promotion.

acceptance_criteria.other: At least 4 of 6 sleeves must individually pass evidence thresholds. At least 2 non-original sleeves must show positive expectancy in at least one non-COVID-recovery era. No single sleeve may contribute more than 40% of total portfolio drawdown.

requirements.notes: Single feed covers all OHLCV-derived signals: trailing returns, momentum ranking, realized volatility, moving averages (SMA for regime filter), RSI, volume. All 36 proposed symbols plus SPY are US-listed equities available on Alpaca. SNOW (IPO Oct 2020), MDB (IPO Oct 2017), COIN (IPO Apr 2021), AFRM (IPO Jan 2021), DDOG (IPO Sep 2019) have shorter histories than the original sleeve members — pre-IPO bars will be absent, reducing era_covid_recovery coverage for these symbols. This is a known limitation, not a blocker.
